User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CineCapture application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

joinDate.description: Date the user joined the application.

Content.title: Content
Content.description: Represents a movie, series, or mini-series recommendation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the content entity.

title.description: Title of the movie or series.

type.description: Type of content (e.g., movie, series, mini-series).

summary.description: Short summary or synopsis of the content.

posterUrl.description: URL of the content's poster image.

status.description: User's status for the content (e.g., watched, unwatched, in progress).

dateAdded.description: Date the content was added to the user's library.

Capture.title: Capture
Capture.description: Represents a screenshot uploaded by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the capture entity.

imageUrl.description: URL of the uploaded screenshot image.

Genre.title: Genre
Genre.description: Represents a genre for movies or series.

id.description: Unique identifier for the genre entity.

name.description: Name of the genre (e.g., Action, Comedy, Drama).

ContentGenre.title: ContentGenre
ContentGenre.description: Represents the many-to-many relationship between Content and Genre.

contentId.description: Reference to Content. (Relationship: Content N:N Genre)

genreId.description: Reference to Genre. (Relationship: Content N:N Genre)

Note.title: Note
Note.description: Represents a user's note or rating for a piece of content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the note entity.

rating.description: User's rating for the content (e.g., on a scale of 1 to 5).

comment.description: User's comment or review of the content.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  Owned by the user identified by {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores movie/series content recommendations for each user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content.

definition.description: Stores screenshots uploaded by the user for specific content. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content.

params.name: captureId
params.description: The unique identifier for the capture.

definition.description: Stores user notes/ratings for content. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the note.

definition.description: Stores the existing genres. Anyone should be able to read it.

params.name: genreId
params.description: The unique identifier for the genre.

definition.description: Stores the relationships between content and genre.

params.name: contentGenreId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content-genre relationship (e.g., a combined contentId and genreId).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support CineCapture's core features: screenshot analysis, metadata enrichment, personal library management, and a Netflix-like interface. The structure prioritizes authorization independence using denormalization and structural segregation to ensure secure and scalable data access. User-specific data is stored in hierarchical paths under the `/users/{userId}` collection, enabling efficient and secure user-based data retrieval. Content, captures, and notes are all organized under the user's document to establish clear ownership and simplify security rules.

Authorization Independence is achieved by denormalizing the `userId` into the `Content`, `Capture`, and `Note` documents. This allows security rules to validate the user's ownership without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. This is crucial for maintaining atomicity in transactions and batches.

The structure supports the QAPs (Rules are not Filters) principle. Each collection contains documents with a homogeneous security posture, making `list` operations secure. Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/contents/{contentId}`) ensures only the authenticated user can access their own content, captures, and notes. Additionally, global roles are not needed as authorization is derived from `request.auth.uid` and `userId` on the documents.
